questions.question: Explain the algorithm for reversing a singly linked list iteratively without creating new nodes. Write the C function.
keyPoints: Use three pointers: previous, current, and next
Iterate through the list reversing pointers
Update pointers appropriately in each iteration
Return the new head (which was the original tail)

questions.question: Explain Floyd's cycle detection algorithm for linked lists with an example.
keyPoints: Two pointers: slow (one step) and fast (two steps)
If they meet, a cycle exists
If fast reaches NULL, no cycle
To find cycle start: reset one pointer to head, move both one step

questions.question: Write a function to concatenate two singly linked lists and a function to merge two sorted linked lists.
keyPoints: Concatenation: traverse first list to end, link to second list head
Merge: compare heads, take smaller, advance pointer
Concatenation time: O(m), Merge time: O(m + n)

questions.question: Explain how merge sort can be applied to a singly linked list. What makes it suitable compared to other sorting algorithms?
keyPoints: Split list using slow/fast pointer to find middle
Time: O(n log n), Space: O(log n) for recursion stack
